raw_text: CFO confirmed a 60,000 EUR budget for the full platform. CTO and procurement team are both attending the demo next Thursday. Need go-live within 8 weeks.
Large logistics company, 500 staff. Board has mandated automation across all ops departments this quarter. CEO contacted us directly. Budget has been signed off.
Fortune 500 retail company. Formal RFP has been issued. Legal and procurement are already engaged. Budget confirmed at 120,000 EUR. Decision expected within 30 days.
Repeat client, currently paying 1,800 EUR per month. Very satisfied with the platform. Wants to add three new automated workflows. Clear upgrade scope, no approval needed.
Series B startup, 80 employees, raised 15M EUR last month. CTO is leading the automation initiative. Full Q2 budget has been approved and allocated.
PE-backed manufacturing firm. Partner sign-off completed last week. Budget confirmed at 80,000 EUR. They want to start immediately — fastest path to contract preferred.
Regional bank with full compliance sign-off. 40,000 EUR pilot budget approved by the CFO. Compliance officer and IT director both confirmed for the scoping call.
Healthcare group managing 12 clinics. Wants to automate patient intake and monthly reporting. Compliance team is involved. Decision maker is the COO.
Insurance company actively switching from a competitor. 30-day internal decision deadline. Operations director is the sponsor. Mid-size contract expected.
200-lawyer firm. Managing partner is directly involved. Formal RFP has been issued. Timeline is 3 months. Budget not yet confirmed but typically 30-50k EUR range.
E-commerce platform at 10,000 orders per day. Technical team is ready. Integration need is clearly defined. CTO wants a decision by end of month.
Global manufacturer has shortlisted our platform. Vendor evaluation is underway. Meeting with VP Operations scheduled for next week. 5 departments to automate.
Pharma company. CIO and compliance officer are both on the evaluation call. Six-figure budget has been approved. Regulatory review is the main dependency.
Telecoms operator has mapped all integration points. 25,000 EUR pilot budget has been confirmed. IT architecture review complete. Awaiting final procurement sign-off.
Senior VP at a large retail chain. Expressed clear interest in automating regional reporting. Mentioned budget availability but no specific figure. Follow-up scheduled.
Operations director at a 500-employee logistics firm. Strong use case around fleet management automation. Budget approval is pending final sign-off from CFO.
Enterprise demo booked for next Tuesday. Contact is head of operations but seniority level unclear. Minimal information provided on use case or company size.
Manager at a mid-size company. Expressed interest in our workflow product. Very vague on timeline. Could not confirm budget. Said they are still in exploration phase.
IT contact at a 500-employee company. Reached out about automation tooling. Unclear if they are the decision maker or just gathering information for their manager.
I am a university student writing my dissertation on AI automation in business. Could you send me information about how your platform works? No commercial intent.
What is your pricing? No company name provided. No stated use case. No contact details beyond the form submission email.
We are a 3-person startup and we are very early stage. Our budget is around 400 EUR. We do not have a specific timeline yet but we like what you do.
I am a solo freelancer and I am interested in automating some of my personal workflows. I do not have a company or team. Looking for something affordable.
High school teacher exploring AI tools for classroom use. Very interested in the technology. Not a business inquiry — personal and educational interest only.
I came across your blog post about workflow automation. Very interesting. I had some general questions about how AI is used in operations. No purchase in mind currently.
I am an intern at a consulting firm and I am building a competitive landscape report on automation platforms. Could you share documentation and pricing details?
NGO project coordinator exploring tools to reduce administrative burden. No dedicated budget. Volunteer-run organisation. Would need significant discount or free tier.
Small property agency, 8 staff. Looking to improve how we handle client follow-ups. Mentioned they have a tool budget. Seemed genuinely interested but gave little detail.
Company inquiry from a local services business. Limited information provided. Owner reached out directly. No specific use case mentioned. Willing to book a call.
Operations manager at a financial services firm. Confirmed decision maker but budget is frozen until Q3. Expressed genuine interest. No current timeline for purchase.
Partner reseller reaching out on behalf of an unnamed client. Indirect revenue opportunity possible. No end-user budget or timeline disclosed. Client has not been briefed yet.
Consultant representing a client they cannot disclose. Budget and timeline completely withheld. Said they would revert with more details after an internal meeting.
Detailed technical questions about API architecture and rate limits. No business need mentioned. No company context. May be a developer doing research or a competitor.
Journalist from a tech publication writing a feature on AI automation. Requesting a comment and access to case studies for editorial purposes. No purchase intent.
Mid-market retail company, 120 staff. Clear automation need around inventory management. Budget is available. However, their operations scale may not match our platform minimum.
Large enterprise with 3,000 staff expressing interest in process automation. Strong engagement on the call. However, their existing tech stack may be incompatible.
Small team of 15 but they have been using automation tools for 3 years. Very sophisticated understanding of ROI. Urgency is clear — current tool contract expires in 60 days.
IT manager with a detailed and well-researched use case for automating their approvals workflow. Strong fit. Budget approval is still pending the CFO but seems likely.
German management consultancy, 150 consultants. CEO is the direct decision maker. Budget confirmed and allocated. Wants to automate internal project reporting.
Property management firm handling 200 properties. Efficiency mandate direct from board. Looking to automate tenant communications and maintenance scheduling.
Legal tech startup, Series A funded. Clear integration need with their existing case management system. CTO engaged directly. Moving fast, want contract in 3 weeks.
Regional retail chain, 50 locations. Centralised operations initiative being driven by CFO. Looking to standardise reporting across all stores. Budget approved.
Recent graduate exploring career in operations. Very interested in AI automation. No company or business context. Looking for learning resources and entry-level tools.
Is there a free tier? No business context provided. Did not mention a company, use case, or team size. Single question submitted via the contact form.
Retired senior executive, personal curiosity about AI. Very knowledgeable about business operations but no longer in an active role. No commercial application.
Manufacturing firm, 300 employees. Automation roadmap approved by board last quarter. CIO is the direct contact. Looking to start scoping within 2 weeks.
Financial services company with a compliance-driven automation requirement. 50,000 EUR budget has been allocated. Compliance officer and IT lead are both engaged.
University researcher studying automation adoption in SMEs. Academic project, no commercial pathway. Looking for anonymised case study data for a journal paper.
Pre-seed startup, still in idea phase. Very early exploration of automation tools. No product-market fit confirmed. No budget, no team beyond 2 co-founders.
Global enterprise, 25,000 staff. Multi-year automation contract under evaluation. CIO and CFO are both named sponsors. Formal procurement process has started.
